CustodianProfile.usersListDescription:
Here, you and your team members can track and manage User validation across all projects. Clicking on a User will show you more information about that User in the linked project.

To **add Users** or to perform User validation on a project-by-project basis, go to the Projects tab above, select a project, and then select the Safe People subtab for that project.

Why are we using the term 'User'? In our engagement with Data Custodians, we found that there was no single 'term' that referred to all the types of people who were accessing sensitive data - researchers, scientists, innovators, engineers, and more. Ultimately, the community landed on 'User' as the all-encompassing term.
CustodianProfile.contactsListDescription: You can use this page to view team members active on this account. Administrators can manage team members and validation, while Approvers can manage validation.
CustodianProfile.organisationsListDescription: This page enables you and your team members to track and manage Organisation validation across all projects.  Clicking on an Organisation will show you more information and any next steps. You can also drag and drop Organisations between the different status lanes, when in 'board view'.
CustodianProfile.searchProjectUsersPlaceholder: Search for User, Project Name, Organisation
CustodianProfile.affiliatedUsers: {num, plural, =0 {No affiliated users} one {# affiliated user} other {# affiliated users}}

rules.info:
The Safe People Registry does not make any decisions as to the final outcomes of which Users or Organisations are validated (considered "safe"). That decision remains with each Data Custodian.

You can still record a User or Organisation as validated to work on a particular project, even if the User/Organisation is flagged as not meeting one of the criteria.

We encourage consulting the [Guidance for TREs assessing safe people and organisations](https://zenodo.org/records/15114755) for community agreed standards in validation.

---

How does IDVT work?

IDVT tests an individual against the following criteria:

1. Valid government issued identification. (Fraud)
2. Likeness check against ID. (Identity)
3. Liveness check. (Identity)
4. Sanctions. (Anti-Money Laundering - AML)

 **Note:** IDVT can currently only be performed by Users residing in the UK.

This feature is one of many supplied as part of Safe People Registry. You do not need to use them all!

validation-checks.info:
These Manual Checks will appear alongside each User and Organisation profile during the validation process. You will be able to navigate between different tabs for the User (Affiliations, Projects, Identify, Training, Automated Flags) and for the Organisation (Users, Address, Digital identifiers, Sector size, Data security compliance) to perform each of these checks manually.

You will always have the option to ultimately record a User or Organisation as validated without following these checks. These checks are a tool for recording decisions only.

We encourage consulting the [Guidance for TREs assessing safe people and organisations](https://zenodo.org/records/15114755) for community agreed standards in validation.

webhooks.info: To register a webhook receiver to consume specific events, provide a publicly accessible URL that can receive HTTP POST requests. Configure the webhook to handle incoming events by parsing the request body. Ensure your endpoint responds with a 2xx status code to acknowledge successful receipt.

integrations.info:
This tool currently imports project information from Gateway.

This only functions if:

- This Custom Integration is enabled on Gateway.
- The Application ID and Client ID match the IDs in the Gateway.

- During each project creation in Safe People Registry, you use a valid Data Use Registry (DUR) ID from within the Gateway

ProfileOrganisation.title: Profile
ProfileOrganisation.companyNotValidPopup: Your organisation has not been validated. This could be related to the information you've entered (i.e. company number). If you believe this message to be in error please contact us at  <contactLink></contactLink>
ProfileOrganisation.companyValidatingPopup: Your organisation is currently being checked against records held by Companies House
ProfileOrganisation.detailsOrganisationSectorDescription: Your Organisation’s sector information should align with Standard Industry Classification (SIC) codes as listed in Companies House or other regulatory sources.
ProfileOrganisation.delegateAdminDescription:
The SRO can invite Delegates to administer the Organisation account. Delegates perform the day-to-day affiliation of Users, as well as invite other Delegates. 

 A Delegate could be a Human Resources (HR) representative, Information Governance (IG) representative e.g. Data Protection Officer (DPO), Contracts representative or Legal representative. 

 You can invite Delegate to register for a Safe People Registry account by adding them below.
ProfileOrganisation.delegateAdminDescriptionDelegate: As a Delegate for your Organisation, you can view all other Delegates.
ProfileOrganisation.uniqueIdentifierCaption: This ‘key’ represents you as an Organisation within Safe People Registry. This is unique to you! Your Organisation will have a unique Safe People Registry identifier associated with your account once your Organisation profile has been completed. This persistent unique identifier will be used to notify Data Custodians of any key changes to your Organisation profile that may impact data access decisions.
ProfileOrganisation.manageResearchersDescription:
As a representative of an Organisation, you have been given permission to associate your affiliated Users (an employee or student of your Organisation) with your Organisation’s Safe People Registry account. Users are individuals involved in active research projects using sensitive data. 
 
Individual Users will create a Safe People Registry account for themselves and will affiliate themselves with an Organisation. 

Automatically invite Users to create a Safe People Registry account. Users will have one Safe People Registry account that will stay with them throughout their career. Select the User(s) you would like to invite to create a Safe People Registry account and select ‘Invite User to create a Safe People Registry account’ in the Actions below.

Identity.idvtCheckDescription:
Some Data Custodians require identity verification before validating you as a User. To streamline approvals across different UK Data Custodians, we recommend completing the identity check in advance. The check is carried out by a third-party provider (Veriff) and includes:

- Verification of a valid government-issued ID (fraud check)

- A likeness check (matching your face to the ID)

- A liveness check (to confirm you're a real, present person)

- A sanctions check (for anti-money laundering purposes)

The Safe People Registry doesn't have access to information uploaded to Veriff - only the results. For more information about Veriff, please consult their [privacy notice](https://www.veriff.com/privacy-notice).
Identity.idvtInvalidCountry: Only users within the UK are permitted to complete the IDVT process.
Identity.emailDescription: <bold>Please use your personal email to set up your Safe People Registry account.</bold> This ensures that your account stays with you, even if you change jobs or leave a university. You'll also need it to recover your Safe People Registry account if you ever lose access.
Identity.locationDescription: Data custodians may need to know which country that you will be accessing data from, as different countries adhere to different laws regarding data sharing and data confidentiality. Also, IDVT below is only available to individuals residing in the UK.
Identity.name: Name

Register.defaultGuidance:
Select an option to view more information about account types.

**Note:** If you hold multiple roles, you'll need a separate account for each.
Register.usersGuidance:
The Safe People Registry is for individuals who need access to sensitive data for research or development. It allows you to create a profile with your identity, training, experience, and affiliations (e.g. an employer or academic institution – referred to as an Organisation in the Safe People Registry).

Your profile isn’t public, but verified UK Data Custodians can view it when assessing data access requests, helping them evaluate the Safe People part of the Five Safes Framework.

Typical Safe People Registry users include researchers, data analysts, innovators, and AI developers from sectors like academia, industry, and the public sector. If you want to:
- setup, view, or edit your profile
- request affiliation from an Organisation
- view your current or past projects

then please choose this option.

**Note**: If you hold multiple roles, you'll need a separate account for each.
Register.organisationsGuidance:
The Safe People Registry helps Organisations that employ or teach researchers or innovators (termed Users) working with sensitive data to create a profile with key information such as identity and data security compliance.

You can:
- View and manage affiliated User profiles
- Notify Data Custodians when a User leaves your Organisation
- See which projects your Users are involved in

Data Custodians use this information to assess the 'safe people' part of the Five Safes Framework. If you're representing an Organisation and want to take any of the above actions, choose this option.

**Note:** If you hold multiple roles, you’ll need a separate account for each.
Register.custodiansGuidance:
The Safe People Registry enables Data Custodian access to User and Organisation profiles, supporting assessment of the Safe People component of the Five Safes Framework.

If you manage secure research data (e.g. via a Trusted Research Environment, Safe Haven, or Secure Data Environment) and need to:
- View or edit your Organisation profile
- Validate Users and Organisations
- Manage projects (past and present)
- Configure User permissions for data access

then please choose this option.

**Note:** If you hold multiple roles, you’ll need a separate account for each.
Register.custodianModalContent: If you are a Data Custodian and would like to use the Safe People Registry, please email us at

Homepage.info: A platform to enable ‘safe people’ decision making
Homepage.supportTitle: The Safe People Registry is a single solution for secure, scalable ‘safe people’ validation.
Homepage.supportUsersInfo:
<bold>Create a single profile</bold>
 so you can be validated for data access
Homepage.supportOrganisationsInfo:
<bold>Create a profile & affiliate Users</bold>
 so you can track who’s working on what
Homepage.supportCustodiansInfo:
<bold>Validate Users & Organisations</bold>
 so you can grant access to the right people
Homepage.usageInfo: With the Safe People Registry, you can:
Homepage.keyFeature1Info: Users and their Organisations can create standardised profiles that make it easier for Data Custodians to validate the safety of a User applying for access to sensitive data.
Homepage.keyFeature2Info: Co-created with Users, Organisations and Data Custodians, the Safe People Registry is an open-source platform that evolves with your needs - <link>share your thoughts</link>!
Homepage.keyFeature3Info: Data Custodians can define their own criteria for rapid validation of Users and Organisations, with customisable features like automated flags and manual checklists.
Homepage.openAccessTitle: Open access and open to listen
Homepage.openAccessContent: We provide this platform free as part of our <openAccessLink>commitment to open access</openAccessLink>. We want everything we build to make it easier for you as Users, Organisations, and Data Custodians. This includes getting your thoughts to inform our design decisions and testing early versions of the product with you so we can stay informed and smoothen your experience. If you'd like to get involved then we'd love you to <getInvolvedLink>get involved</getInvolvedLink>.

About.mission: Our mission is to enable Users, Organisations and Data Custodians to collaborate efficiently, saving time and supporting secure data access while upholding the highest standards of security and compliance.
About.backgroundParagraph1: Research can transform lives, but often requires the use of highly sensitive information, which must only be accessed by accredited professionals, in secure environments, and for projects that benefit the public. These principles are captured under the <fiveSafesLink>Five Safes Framework</fiveSafesLink> which guide the responsible use of sensitive data in research.
About.backgroundParagraph2: The Safe People Registry is a secure software solution designed to standardise and simplify User (researcher, innovator) validation for secure data access – the ‘safe people’ principle. It is maintained and supported by Health Data Research UK’s <techLink>Technology Team</techLink>, but is not health-data specific.
About.backgroundImageAlt: Five Safes Framework diagram showing Safe projects, Safe settings, Safe people, Safe data, and Safe outputs.
About.historyParagraph1: The HDR UK Tech Team knew both in theory and often personally how frustrating and fragmented the process of accessing sensitive data could be — especially when researchers and organisations had to repeatedly prove their credibility. And this wasn’t just a health‑sector issue; the same challenge showed up in education and beyond. We therefore applied for and gained funding from UKRI and DSIT in 2023 to come up with a solution.
About.historyParagraph2: We engaged heavily with stakeholders, running our ideas past the <panUkLink>Pan-UK Data Governance Steering Group</panUkLink>, the <ukTreLink>UK Trusted Research Environment Community</ukTreLink>, and more. There was no standard system, guidance, or tool for ‘safe people’ validations. In response, we engaged in a multi-pronged approach to understand the landscape:
About.historyItem1: Listening closely to the needs of data users and access approvers
About.historyItem3: Supporting the development of guidance (see key references below)
About.historyOutro: The result is the Safe People Registry: a flexible and practical tool designed to simplify ‘safe people’ validations and support consistent, confident decision making, even as needs and policies evolve over time.
About.historyImageAlt: Collage of people working collaboratively on research and data.
About.openAccessTitle: Open access and open to listen
About.openAccessText: We provide this platform free as part of our <openAccessLink>commitment to open access</openAccessLink>. We want everything we build to make it easier for you as Users, Organisations, and Data Custodians. This includes getting your thoughts to inform our design decisions and testing early versions of the product with you so we can stay informed and smoothen your experience. If you’d like to get involved then we’d love you to <getInvolvedLink>get involved</getInvolvedLink>.
About.keyReference1: Guidance for TREs assessing safe people and organisations

FAQ.mission: Our mission is to enable Users, Organisations and Data Custodians to collaborate efficiently, saving time and supporting secure data access while upholding the highest standards of security and compliance.
FAQ.howSecureQ: How does the Safe People Registry keep personal information secure?
FAQ.howSecureA:
The Safe People Registry does not, itself, host any of the secure data from the Data Custodians. All datasets remain with the Data Custodians. The Safe People Registry does collect personal information in the profiles of Users and Organisations, which can be seen when added to projects by Data Custodians. 
The security in our system ensures that specific User and Organisation details remain strictly viewable by those who administer those individual accounts and the Data Custodians. Researchers must approve/deny access to their Safe People Registry data should a Data Custodian wish to implement software measures within their own systems. These requests will only ever come from known Data Custodian’s within the Safe People Registry, and all API access enforces signed requests/responses to ensure data isn’t tampered with in transit. 
The security in our system ensures that specific User and Organisation details remain strictly viewable by those who administer those individual accounts and the Data Custodians. Researchers must approve/deny access to their Safe People Registry data should a Data Custodian wish to implement software measures within their own systems. These requests will only ever come from known Data Custodian’s within the Safe People Registry, and all API access enforces signed requests/responses to ensure data isn’t tampered with in transit.
FAQ.multipleOrgQ: Can I be associated with more than one Organisation?
FAQ.multipleOrgA: Can I be associated with more than one Organisation?
FAQ.howKeepPersonalInfoSecureQ: How does the Safe People Registry keep personal information secure?
howKeepPersonalInfoSecureA: The Safe People Registry does not, itself, host any of the secure data from the Data Custodians. All datasets remain with the Data Custodians. The Safe People Registry does collect personal information in the profiles of Users and Organisations, which can be seen when added to projects by Data Custodians. The security in our system ensures that specific User and Organisation details remain strictly viewable by the account owners themselves, their Organisations, and the Data Custodians.

Users must approve/deny access to their Safe People Registry data should a Data Custodian wish to implement software measures within their own systems. These requests will only ever come from known Data Custodian’s within the Safe People Registry, and all API access enforces signed requests/responses to ensure data isn’t tampered with in transit.
<bold>Penetration testing</bold>: We have automated vulnerability testing weekly, which feed immediately into our development cycle.
FAQ.orgValidationSafeSettingQ: How does Organisation validation in the Safe People Registry relate to the ‘safe setting’ in the Five Safe framework?
FAQ.orgValidationSafeSettingA: In the Safe People Registry, Organisation validation if performed by the Data Custodian, and confirms that an organisation is legitimate and has sufficient security accreditation for its Users (staff, students) to access sensitive data. The ‘safe setting’ in the Five Safes framework refers to the technical and security controls of the environment where data is accessed, such as cybersecurity protections and data handling policies. Sometimes, that environment is managed by the User’s Organisation - necessitating Organisation validation. But increasingly, data stays within a secure platform managed by the Data Custodian, and Users access it remotely.
FAQ.healthDataResearchOnlyQ: Since Health Data Research UK developed this tool, is it only for health data research?
FAQ.healthDataResearchOnlyA: Not at all. The Safe People Registry is designed to work with any type of data. While it was developed by Health Data Research UK, we collaborated with experts from a range of fields, including health, education, social science, economics, and more, to ensure it’s flexible and widely applicable.
FAQ.orgAddedRegistryQ: How does an Organisation get added to the Safe People Registry?
FAQ.orgAddedRegistryA: A Senior Responsible Officer (SRO) from the Organisation creates a profile in the Safe People Registry and uploads the necessary documents (including signed approval from their Organisation that the SRO has the relevant authority to undertake the role). Once submitted, each Data Custodian can review the information and decide whether they recognise the Organisation as ‘safe’.
validationApprovalAffiliationDiffA: <bold>Validation:</bold> A Data Custodian confirms that a User or Organisation meets their requirements to be considered ‘safe’.

<bold>Approval:</bold> A Data Custodian records that approval has been given for a specific project. Project approval processes take place outside of the Safe People Registry often by a Data Governance Approval Committee. The Safe People Registry enables a Data Custodian to record a project approval.
<bold>Affiliation:</bold> An Organisation takes legal responsibility for a User, often as their employer or sponsor.
FAQ.whoUploadsProjectInfoQ: Who is responsible for uploading project information?
FAQ.whoUploadsProjectInfoA: The Data Custodian uploads project details using information gathered from processes outside the Safe People Registry, such as data access requests. Once a project is created, the Data Custodian can then add Users to the project.
FAQ.validationGrantAccessQ: Does validation on the Safe People Registry automatically grant me access?
FAQ.validationGrantAccessA: No. The Safe People Registry is used by Data Custodians to record validation decisions only. It does not automatically grant access to data. Projects must be provided separately and recorded as approved by the Data Custodian. A project could potentially be recorded as approved by a Data Custodian, but an individual User not validated to work on the project.
FAQ.projectApprovedUserValidatedQ: Can a project be recorded as approved without a User being validated? Or can a User be validated without the project being recorded as approved?
FAQ.projectApprovedUserValidatedA: Yes, both are possible! The Safe People Registry keeps track of these decisions separately. Some Data Custodians review and record approvals on projects and validations of Users one after the other, while others do both at the same time. Our platform is flexible to support either approach.
FAQ.learnMoreSafePeopleQ: Where can I learn more about ‘safe people’ validations and the concepts that underpin this system?
FAQ.learnMoreSafePeopleA: You can find helpful information and important resources in the ‘key references’ section on our About page and the <explainerVideo>explainer video</explainerVideo>.
FAQ.multiOrganisationQ: Can I be associated with more than one Organisation?
FAQ.multiOrganisationA: Yes! You can be linked to multiple organisations in the system, making it easy to manage your roles across different teams.
FAQ.personalEmailLoginQ: Why do I need to login with my personal email?
FAQ.personalEmailLoginA: Using your personal email lets you keep your Safe People Registry profile no matter which Organisation you work with. When you join or change Organisations, you simply link your professional email to your profile to complete the affiliation process.
FAQ.userSaveTimeQ: How does the Safe People Registry save me time?
FAQ.userSaveTimeA: The Safe People Registry stores your ‘safe people’ credentials in one secure, standard profile. This means when you apply to access sensitive data, Data Custodians can quickly verify your details, speeding up the whole process. Some additional time-saving benefits include: ORCiD integration, automatic notifications, and optional identity verification done once.
FAQ.linkHRSystemsQ: Can we link the Safe People Registry to our internal HR systems?
FAQ.linkHRSystemsA: Yes! The Safe People Registry can connect with your internal HR systems, by using automated API links.
FAQ.sroRoleQ: What is the role of the Senior Responsible Officer?
FAQ.sroRoleA: The SRO is responsible for setting up the Organisation’s record and making sure all the information stays accurate and up to date.
FAQ.sroDelegateQ: Can the SRO delegate affiliations to someone else?
FAQ.sroDelegateA: Yes. Once setting up the Organisation’s profile, the SRO can appoint trusted Organisational administrators (termed <bold>Delegates</bold> in the Safe People Registry) who can manage User affiliations on their behalf.
FAQ.orgSeeProjectsBeforeApprovalQ: Can Organisations see projects they are associated with before the projects are recorded as approved?
FAQ.orgSeeProjectsBeforeApprovalA: Yes! The Organisation can also see in real time if/when the Data Custodian records project approval.
FAQ.orgVerifiedQ: How are Organisations verified to use the Safe People Registry?
FAQ.orgVerifiedA: The Organisation creates their profile and fills in important information like their SRO details, as well as submitting a signed SRO declaration. This is checked by members of the Safe People Registry Team (which usually takes <1 week), before full Organisation access is granted.
FAQ.registryMakeDecisionsQ: Does the Safe People Registry make decisions?
FAQ.registryMakeDecisionsA: No. The Safe People Registry does not decide whether a project is approved or whether a User or Organisation is validated. All decisions are made solely by the Data Custodian responsible for the data.
FAQ.automatedFlagsManualChecksQ: Why are there both automated flags and manual checks? How do they relate to the validation process?
automatedFlagsManualChecksA: The Safe People Registry itself doesn’t decide whether someone is a ‘safe’ User. That decision is always made by a person. To help with this, Data Custodians can use a manual checklist to review each criterion, marking pass or fail and adding comments as needed.

Automated flags speed up the process by highlighting certain information automatically, such as a User’s location or whether they have completed specific training. These flags don’t make the decision for you, they simply make it quicker and easier to review the relevant details during validation.
FAQ.registryDataAccessAppsQ: How does the Safe People Registry interact with data access applications, such as the submission of a data access request?
FAQ.registryDataAccessAppsA: The ‘safe people’ validation check can happen at the same time as a data access request. By using the Safe People Registry, you can speed things up, for example, by including a Safe People Registry ID field in your data access request form (or the equivalent process used by your Data Custodian). If you use the <hdrLink>Health Data Research Gateway</hdrLink>, there are built-in integrations to make this even easier.
FAQ.usersAddedProjectsQ: How do Users get added to projects?
FAQ.usersAddedProjectsA: Only Data Custodians can add Users to projects. If your Organisation needs you to be added, they can make a request to the Data Custodian on your behalf. Users cannot add themselves to projects.
FAQ.auditLogsManagedA: The Safe People Registry keeps a record of all User actions for security and transparency. Data Custodians can view logs showing any updates to User profiles, as well as changes in their affiliation made by their Organisation.
FAQ.delegateProjectTasksQ: Can I delegate project approvals and validation tasks to other staff members in my organisation?
delegateProjectTasksA: Yes. You can add colleagues as Team Members in your Data Custodian profile. They can be assigned as:
Administrators: able to update your profile settings and configurations
Approvers: able to review and record approval of projects, and validate Users or Organisations.
FAQ.fillFiveSafesQ: Do I need to fill in all ‘Five Safes’ details for every project?
FAQ.fillFiveSafesA: No. To get started, you only need to add a project title and summary before you can begin adding Users. however, the additional project information fields are there to match the UK Health Data Research Alliance’s <dataTransparency>Data Access Transparency standards</dataTransparency>. Completing these fields will allow you to automatically create a public-facing Data Use Register without extra work.
FAQ.custodianSaveTimeQ: How does the Safe People Registry save me time?
custodianSaveTimeA: The Safe People Registry keeps all the key information, validations, and decisions about a User’s or Organisation’s ‘safeness’ in one secure, central place. This means fewer emails, less chasing, and faster validations. Some additional benefits include:
User and Organisation profiles in a clear, standard format
Automatic generation of a public Data Use Register, instantly generated from project information
Notifications for validations status changes and expiring training certificates
Automatic flags to make validations quick and easy
FAQ.registryStopSupportedQ: What happens if the Safe People Registry stop being supported?
FAQ.registryStopSupportedA: This is highly unlikely to happen, as we have long term funding as an institute to support the system. Nevertheless, there is no vendor lock-in. You can use the API to pull out all your information, and detailed assistance for how to do this will be supplied if ever the service had to shut down, or if you wish to change systems due to your own preferences.

administrator.definition: An individual who configures a Data Custodian’s settings in the Safe People Registry and can add Approvers.

affliation.definition:
In the Safe People Registry, this is the formal process where an Organisation confirms that:
• There is a verified link (e.g. employment contract, honorary contract, or student enrolment) between the User and Organisation.
• The Safe People Registry User profile accurately matches the employee or student.
• The Organisation approves the User to access sensitive data.
• The User’s organisational email matches their official email address within the Organisation.

approval.definition: The decision by a Data Custodian that a project meets all necessary safety and compliance requirements, allowing them to share the requested sensitive data.

approver.definition: An individual who can review and approve User/Organisations validations and record project approvals.

dataCustodian.definition:
In the Safe People Registry, a Data Custodian is an organisation that hosts and controls a Trusted Research Environment (TRE), Secure Data Environment (SDE), Safe Haven, or a team providing access to a research dataset.

Data Custodians are responsible for nominating Administrators to manage their Safe People Registry settings and input Data Custodian-specific information. They also nominate Approvers to make ‘safe people’ validation decisions based on information provided by Users and Organisations.

delegates.definition: Individuals from an Organisation, appointed by the SRO, who can affiliate Users with the Organisation but cannot make changes to the Organisation’s profile.

organisation.definition: This is the university, institution, commercial research organisation, administrative body or other organisation that a User belongs to. An Organisation is ultimately responsible for the behaviour of their User requesting access to data from a Data Custodian.

safeHaven.definition: Are similar to TREs. One example is the <safeHavenLink>Scottish Safe Haven Network</safeHavenLink>.

secureDataEnvironment.definition: Are similar to TREs. One example is the <nhsLink>NHS</nhsLink>. 'The Secure Data Environment (SDE) is a secure data and research analysis platform. It is part of an interoperable NHS Research Secure Data Environment network.'

seniorResponsibleOfficer.definition: The person within an Organisation who creates and manages its Safe People Registry profile. The SRO can also appoint other administrative delegates.

sponsorship.definition: The formal process where an Organisation takes legal responsibility for a project.

trustedResearchEnvironment.definition: A secure digital platform that enables researchers and innovators to access sensitive data in a way that protects privacy and maintains public trust. TREs are designed to ensure that data is used safely, ethically, and legally, typically by providing controlled access, audit trails, and governance frameworks.

user.definition: A User may be an employee, student, or hold an honorary contract with an Organisation. In the Safe People Registry, Users create a profile containing details about their identity, research experience, training, and accreditations, and can request affiliation from their Organisation. This information is used by Data Custodians to validate their ‘safe people’ status. Each User account is linked to a unique identifier, allowing it to remain with the individual even if they change Organisations.

validatedUser.definition: This is equivalent to the “Approved Researcher” (as defined in the standard DAA and the Safe People Guidance). A User accesses and uses the data in a TRE/SDE/Safe Haven for Approved Project(s).

validation.definition: The process by which a Data Custodian confirms that a User or Organisation meets the required criteria for being considered ‘safe’.

HowItWorks.intro:
‘Safe people’ validation is a key step in ensuring secure access to sensitive data. The Safe People Registry is a secure platform designed to facilitate this validation process.

 Our mission is to enable Users, Organisations and Data Custodians to collaborate efficiently, saving time and supporting secure data access while upholding the highest standards of security and compliance.
HowItWorks.keyImageAlt: Illustration of a secure digital registry with a user access icon and connected network nodes.
HowItWorks.infoUsers: A User (researcher, innovator, analyst, engineer, etc.) creates and manages one profile that supports access to sensitive data across Data Custodians.

saveTime.title: Save time and reduce duplication
saveTime.body: Create your profile once and share it with multiple Data Custodians. No need to repeat the same steps for every project.

fastSetup.title: Fast setup
fastSetup.body: Connect your profile using familiar tools like ORCiD to speed up the process.

multipleOrganisations.title: Multiple Organisations
multipleOrganisations.body: You can work or study with multiple Organisations, and easily track which affiliation is used for which project.

keepEverything.title: Keep everything in one place
keepEverything.body: Track your validation status and training credentials centrally.

automaticUpdates.title: Automatic updates
automaticUpdates.body: Get notified when your validation status changes or when training certificates are about to expire.

builtToMove.title: Built to move with you
builtToMove.body: Your profile stays with you, even when you move between organisations or institutions, making it easy to keep your information up to date.

HowItWorks.infoOrganisations: Organisations employ or educate Users working on sensitive data projects. Organisations affiliate and track their Users with smart, secure management.

saveTime.title: Affiliate once
saveTime.body: Affiliate Users quickly and confidently. No need to repeat the process for each new project or data access request.

fastSetup.title: Save time and reduce duplication
fastSetup.body: Create your profile once and share it with multiple Data Custodians. No need to repeat the same steps for every project.

multipleOrganisations.title: Full visibility
multipleOrganisations.body: Track affiliated Users in one place. See who they are, what projects they’re working on, and which Data Custodians they’re connected with.

automaticUpdates.title: Smart notifications
automaticUpdates.body: If you record when a User leaves your Organisation, all relevant Data Custodians are informed, keeping everyone up to date.

builtToMove.title: Sponsorships
builtToMove.body: In the health and care data field, you can optionally sponsor projects by recording formal oversight of research.

HowItWorks.infoCustodians: Data Custodians make informed decisions about granting access to sensitive data faster, easier, and while ensuring compliance.

saveTime.title: Easy tracking
saveTime.body: Keep all ‘safe people’ validation information, decisions and statuses in one secure place.

fastSetup.title: Team-wide visibility
fastSetup.body: Share validation decisions, comments, and supporting information across your team, making it easier to collaborate on tasks and maintain.

multipleOrganisations.title: Automation
multipleOrganisations.body: Set minimum validation requirements and let the platform automatically flag missing information, saving time and helping you stay aligned to your data governance policies.

automaticUpdates.title: Smart notifications
automaticUpdates.body: Receive alerts when a User changes Organisation, updates their profile, completes training, or when a project ends.

builtToMove.title: Shared intelligence
builtToMove.body: See which other Data Custodians and projects a User is involved with to help inform your decisions.

flexibleFeatures.title: Flexible optional features
flexibleFeatures.body: Choose to enable features like Single Sign-on for access, project approval recording, or automatic publishing of project details to a public data use register. Whatever works best for you!

HowItWorks.videoIntro: Take a deeper dive into the products with our demos

InfoHero.title: The Safe People Registry is <bold>secure</bold>, <bold>supported</bold>, <bold>open-source</bold> and <bold>free</bold>
InfoHero.content:
We co-created the Safe People Registry with Users, Organisations and Data Custodians to make ‘safe people’ validation faster, easier, and more secure.

Led by Health Data Research UK’s (HDR UK) <techLink>Technology Team</techLink> (though not health-data specific), the Safe People Registry is a free, open-source platform designed for long-term use and built with collaboration at its core.

The Safe People Registry continues to evolve in response to your needs. <helpLink>Help us develop further!</helpLink>

GetInvolved.intro1: The Safe People Registry was created in collaboration with Data Custodians, Organisations, and Users from across the UK's sensitive data community. From the very beginning, we've believed in the power of open-source development and community-driven innovation.
GetInvolved.intro2: We welcome everyone, whether you're already using the Safe People Registry or just curious about it, to get involved, share ideas, and help shape its future.
GetInvolved.intro3: Here's how you can get involved:
GetInvolved.mailingListDescription: Hear about our latest features, events, & user testing invitations!
GetInvolved.feedbackDescription: Have an idea? Want to comment on or help with our roadmap?
GetInvolved.testFeatureDescription: We're working on how our Notifications system functions.
GetInvolved.surveyDescription: Tell us your priorities by filling in our roadmap survey.
GetInvolved.acknowledgementTitle: Acknowledgement to key groups who have already been instrumental in the co-creation of our software:
GetInvolved.acknowledgementItem1: Alliance members including members of the Pan-UK Data Governance Steering Group
GetInvolved.acknowledgementItem2: Representatives from the NHS Data for Research and Development Programme and NHS Research Secure Data Environment Network
GetInvolved.acknowledgementItem3: Representatives from the UK Trusted Research Environment Community
GetInvolved.acknowledgementItem4: Representatives from the HDR UK Public Advisory Board

ContactUs.intro1: We'd love to hear from you!
ContactUs.intro2: Whether you have a question, spot a bug, or want to share your ideas, we’re here to help.
ContactUs.questionDescription: You may also find our FAQs section helpful.
ContactUs.helpDescription: Something not working as you expect, or a frustrating experience? We want to help.
ContactUs.getInvolvedDescription: Be part of the conversation and help shape the future of the Safe People Registry. You can hear about events, take part in user testing, and get early updates on new features.
ContactUs.emailDescription: Finally, you can always email us at <emailLink>enquiries@safepeopleregistry.org</emailLink> if you have thoughts not covered by the above!
